title: Ctenitis aripensis
taxonomy: Ctenitis aripensis is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae, a group of ferns. This species belongs to the genus Ctenitis, which is a genus of ferns native to tropical and subtropical regions. Ctenitis aripensis is further classified as an evergreen epiphytic fern, indicating its growth habit and habitat preferences. The species is native to South America, where it is found growing on trees and other vegetation. Ctenitis aripensis is a relatively rare species within its genus, and its classification is essential for understanding its unique characteristics and conservation status.

intro: Ctenitis aripensis is a species of fern native to South America, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae and commonly known as aripensis fern. Traditionally, Ctenitis aripensis is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and modulating the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis aripensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Ctenitis aripensis is typically used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly consumed to take advantage of its health benefits.
